DECLARATION OF ANA GONZALEZ IN SUPPORT OF EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER FILED BY PETITIONER KIRCHMEYER, KIMBERLY , DIRECTOR, DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS, STATE OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. NO APPEARANCES. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THE PETITION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING RESPONDENTS KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. AND SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP TO COMPLY WITH THE INVESTIGATIONAL SUBPOENAS ISSUED BY THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA IS GRANTED. RESPONDENTS DO NOT RAISE ANY ARGUMENT ON THE MERITS IN OPPOSITION TO THE PETITION, BUT MERELY INDICATE THAT THEY WILL COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SUBPOENAS IN QUESTION WERE REGULARLY ISSUED AND SERVED AND THAT DR. LOPEZ'S OBJECTIONS ARE MERITLESS AND DO NOT JUSTIFY WITHHOLDING THE SUBPOENAED RECORDS. ACCORDINGLY, THEY MUST BE PRODUCED WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Off Calendar Apr-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Order To Show Cause.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

The petition for an order compelling Respondents Kaiser Foundation Health Plan Inc. and Southern California Permanente Medical Group to comply with the investigational subpoenas issued by the Medical Board of California is granted. Respondents do not raise any argument on the merits in opposition to the petition, but merely indicate that they will comply with the Court's order. The Court finds that the subpoenas in question were regularly issued and served and that Dr. Lopez's objections are meritless and do not justify withholding the subpoenaed records. Accordingly, they must be produced within 10 days of notice of entry of the Court's order.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
